segments.text: Now king David was old and stricken in years; and they covered him with clothes, but he gat no heat.
Wherefore his servants said unto him,
Let there be sought for my lord the king a young virgin: and let her stand before the king, and let her cherish him, and let her lie in thy bosom, that my lord the king may get heat.
segments.speaker: David's Servants

footnotes: Let there…: Heb. Let them seek
a young…: Heb. a damsel, a virgin
cherish…: Heb. be a cherisher unto him

segments.text: So they sought for a fair damsel throughout all the coasts of Israel, and found Abishag a Shunammite, and brought her to the king.
And the damsel was very fair, and cherished the king, and ministered to him: but the king knew her not.
Then Adonijah the son of Haggith exalted himself,
and he prepared him chariots and horsemen, and fifty men to run before him.
Wherefore Nathan spake unto Bath-sheba the mother of Solomon,
Hast thou not heard that Adonijah the son of Haggith doth reign, and David our lord knoweth it not?
segments.speaker: Nathan

segments.text: My lord, O king, hast thou said, Adonijah shall reign after me, and he shall sit upon my throne?
segments.speaker: Nathan

segments.text: As the LORD liveth, that hath redeemed my soul out of all distress,
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: If he will shew himself a worthy man, there shall not an hair of him fall to the earth: but if wickedness shall be found in him, he shall die.
segments.speaker: Solomon
